name: eruto-skills
description: Claude Code skills for research, presentations, and creative writing

plugins.name: research-note
plugins.description: Research any topic from web, YouTube transcripts, PDFs, X/Twitter posts, or GitHub repos, then generate a footnoted Markdown note with an optional Mermaid mindmap. Includes source-quality tiers and headless-Chrome fetching.

plugins.name: marp
plugins.description: Create, edit, and manage Marp (Markdown) presentation slides. Five modes (create/edit/theme/convert/read) with a design-first approach, layout pattern library, and 60-30-10 color system.

plugins.name: presentation
plugins.description: Plan a presentation end-to-end before implementation. Triages requirements, designs the story arc, generates a slide outline, and runs structure QA — then routes the build to a marp or pptx skill. Includes structure-pattern library, content-strategy reference, QA checklist, and outline template.

plugins.name: slide-visual-reviewer
plugins.description: Verify slide visual quality from PDF/PNG output. Detects text overflow, element overlap, margin issues, and other layout problems via visual inspection of PNG images. Designed as a sub-skill invoked from marp/pptx QA workflows, but also usable standalone.

plugins.name: nanj-thread
plugins.description: Generate a Japanese anonymous-forum (なんJ / 2ch / 5ch) style thread where fictitious commenters observe and riff on the user, a project, or a given topic. For creative-writing and parody use.

plugins.name: analyze-video
plugins.description: Analyze a YouTube reference video's structure and rhetorical techniques. Fetches metadata and subtitles via yt-dlp, then extracts macro/micro structure, hooks, pacing, hedging language, persuasion patterns, retention tactics, and memorable phrases as a Markdown report. Compare two videos via --compare.

plugins.name: review-content
plugins.description: Review the content quality of a video, presentation, or long-form narration on 18 axes — structure (hook / SCQA / logic / pacing), content (accuracy / coverage / specificity / redundancy), delivery (clarity / audience-fit / engagement / emotion / memorable phrases / visual sync), persuasion (credibility / counterargument handling / takeaway / title match). Outputs an A/B/C/D scorecard with concrete improvements.

plugins.name: autopilot
plugins.description: Scaffold long-running, hands-off Claude Code sessions. Generates a plan with user-observable acceptance criteria, a deterministic loop body, cross-iteration lesson memory, and hard budget/iteration ceilings — then hands off to /loop (interactive) or /schedule (remote cron). Halts on uncertainty instead of guessing.

plugins.name: design-deai
plugins.description: Detect AI slop patterns (purple gradients, Inter font fixation, rounded-2xl overuse, glassmorphism, missing Empty/Error states, absent design tokens) in UI source code via static analysis. Calculates a slop score (0–100) and generates a DESIGN.md to prevent recurring AI-generated anti-patterns.

plugins.name: design-brief
plugins.description: Define visual design direction for a Remotion presentation. Reads the content outline, analyzes emotional arc and visual metaphors, proposes 2–3 design concepts (color palette, typography, animation language, mood), and generates a theme TypeScript file.

plugins.name: ui-visual-reviewer
plugins.description: Audit a web app's rendered UI colors using getComputedStyle. Calculates WCAG AA/AAA contrast ratios and validates against design tokens — captures opacity-stacked and CSS-variable-resolved colors that screenshot review misses.

plugins.name: fetch-web
plugins.description: Fetch web page content as Markdown using a tiered strategy: r.jina.ai (primary), headless Chrome with Puppeteer (fallback), direct curl (last resort). Shared utility referenced by other skills for URL content retrieval.

plugins.name: fetch-asset
plugins.description: Download media files from the web using the right tool for each source: yt-dlp for video/audio (1000+ sites), gallery-dl for image galleries (Pixiv, Twitter/X, etc.), a Pixabay CDN extractor script, aria2 for parallel downloads, and curl for direct links.
